title: Canadian Overnight Repo Rate Average
summary: The Bank of Canada publishes daily CORRA (Canadian Overnight Repo Rate Average) data, which serves as Canada's risk-free reference rate. The most recent published CORRA values show rates ranging from 2.27% to 2.33% across the week of March 24-30, 2026, with total daily dollar trading volume between approximately $47.5 billion and $57.4 billion.
key_points: CORRA is Canada's risk-free rate measuring the cost of overnight general collateral funding in Canadian dollars using Government of Canada securities as collateral
Latest published CORRA rates: March 24 at 2.30%, March 25 at 2.33%, March 26 at 2.32%, March 27 at 2.29%, March 30 at 2.27%
Total eligible trading volume ranged from $47.50B to $57.42B daily during the observed period
Number of unique data submitters remained stable at 14-15 participants daily
Trimmed dollar volume (after removing outliers) ranged from $35.62B to $43.06B
CORRA transparency metrics include percentile distributions (5th, 25th, 75th, 95th) and trimmed volume rates
why_it_matters: CORRA serves as Canada's equivalent to SOFR and is used to calculate interest for floating rate notes, loans, and derivatives. Market participants use CORRA data to assess short-term funding conditions in Canadian dollar markets. The stability of 14-15 data submitters and the narrow spread between percentiles indicates healthy market functioning.
macro_context: CORRA represents the cost of overnight secured borrowing in Canadian dollars and serves as a benchmark for broader monetary conditions. The observed rates of 2.27-2.33% are consistent with the Bank of Canada's current monetary policy stance. This is raw reference rate data without commentary on policy implications.
market_structure_context: CORRA is calculated from overnight repo transactions using Government of Canada securities as collateral. The data includes trimmed volume calculations that remove outliers to produce a more stable rate. The 14-15 unique data submitters represent the core dealer community contributing to this benchmark.
important_numbers: CORRA Compounded Index base value 100 on June 12, 2020
CORRA Compounded Index values 115.935-115.979 for March 25-31, 2026
notes: This is a daily data publication page from the Bank of Canada, not an analytical article or policy commentary. The page provides raw CORRA values and transparency metrics for download. CORRA is a legitimate macro/market structure data point as Canada's risk-free overnight rate benchmark. The data is current (within days of TODAY) and from a high-trust domain. No policy signals or commentary are present—only raw numbers and methodology references.
